description: This file defines negation in the Past Continuous tense in Njo Viri (Balanda). Past continuous negation expresses actions that were not ongoing at a specific point in the past. Two negation strategies are grammatically licensed.

general_principle.statement: Past continuous negation in Balanda preserves the progressive marker 'ti-' and negates the clause using either the discontinuous marker 'maka … gba' or the preverbal negator 'kawilla'.

constraints: maka must appear before the ti-verb
examples: Ne maka timoliki gbi difo gba

constraints: gba must not be used with kawilla

aspect_preservation.rule: The progressive prefix 'ti-' must be retained in all negated past continuous forms.
constraints: ti- cannot be omitted under negation
negation does not change aspectual meaning

word_order_rules.rule: Negation markers do not alter core word order.

error_constraints: Do not combine 'maka' and 'kawilla' in the same clause
Do not omit 'ti-' in past continuous negation
Do not insert 'noka' in negated clauses
Do not move 'gba' from sentence-final position

english: I was not eating fish
She was not drinking water

balanda: Danda maka titeli muru famvara gba
english: We were not sleeping on the bed
Uku was not going
Ngande was not coming home
They were not walking in the compound
She was not running fast
Uku was not working in the garden

balanda: Wiki maka timoliki gbi difo gba
english: The child was not playing in the compound
I was not watching the dog

balanda: Ne maka tivele njo balanda gba
english: I was not speaking Balanda
The girl was not writing words
I was not reading a book
He was not thinking about it
They were not knowing it
I was not asking them

balanda: Ngande maka tivele zi ndi gba
english: Ngande was not telling them

balanda: Wilii kawilla tikeye ti naa ni
english: The girl was not calling her mother
I was not loving you
He was not fearing the snake
She was not hating you
Ngande was not surprising him
He was not seeming calm
The girl was not tying the shoe
He was not cutting the pot
I was not pulling the door
We were not pushing the box
The girl was not washing the shoe
I was not hiding the beads
Ngande was not building a house
I was not creating words
She was not changing the words
I was not turning the door
We were not adding money
I was not describing Balanda
They were not helping the poor
Ngande was not initiating the project
I was not contributing to the project
The girl was not separating trash
We were not upholding the law
I was not wanting water
She was not giving money
I was not finding money
Uku was not trying the cloth
Ngande was not leaving home
I was not arriving in Wau
He was not sharpening the knife
Ngande was not imitating adults
We were not starting the project
He was not falling
